content.problemStatement:
Consider an array BBB of length MMM.

We define the prefix maximum array of array BBB, F(B)F(B)F(B), as an array CCC of length MMM such that Ci=max⁡(B1,B2,…Bi)C_i=\max(B_1, B_2, \ldots B_i)Ci​=max(B1​,B2​,…Bi​).

Stack had a permutation PPP of length 2⋅N2 \cdot N2⋅N. He deleted the last NNN elements from PPP to obtain an array PPP of size NNN.

Stack performs the following step for **all** permutations QQQ of length 2⋅N2 \cdot N2⋅N :


Check whether Qi=PiQ_i = P_iQi​=Pi​ for all 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N;
If the above condition is true, insert F(Q)F(Q)F(Q) in set ZZZ.
Find the resultant size of set ZZZ.

Since the value might be large, please print it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

Note that a permutation of length 2⋅N2\cdot N2⋅N contains all integers from 111 to 2⋅N2\cdot N2⋅N exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the current length of permutation PPP.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers P1,P2,…PNP_1, P_2, \ldots P_NP1​,P2​,…PN​ representing the first NNN elements of PPP.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the size of set ZZZ modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: Pi≠PjP_i \neq P_jPi​=Pj​ for 1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.
